title: Implement a Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time. This exercise teaches you how to implement data structures with optimal time complexity.
instructions: Create a class named `MinStack` with the following methods:
push(val): Push element val onto the stack
pop(): Remove the element on top of the stack
getMin(): Retrieve the minimum element in the stack in O(1) time
All operations should work in O(1) time complexity
learningObjectives: Learn to optimize for time complexity
Master the concept of auxiliary data structures

hints.content: To track the minimum in O(1) time, you need an auxiliary data structure. Consider using a second stack that keeps track of minimum values.

Maintain two stacks: one for all elements and one for minimums. When pushing, add to minStack only if the value is <= current minimum. When popping, remove from minStack if the value equals current minimum.
Implementation: this.stack for values, this.minStack for minimums. Push to minStack when val <= minStack.top. Pop from minStack when popped value === minStack.top. getMin returns minStack.top.
